assertion_results.text: Response uses a cache key pattern that includes both a userId component and a YYYY-MM-DD date string
assertion_results.evidence: Key is `oracle_${date}` — no userId component by default (only mentioned as an afterthought: 'you could prefix with user ID'). Date is NOT YYYY-MM-DD format — uses toLocaleDateString() which produces '6/26/2026' (locale-dependent, not sortable)

assertion_results.text: Response uses localStorage.getItem and localStorage.setItem as the storage mechanism
assertion_results.evidence: localStorage.getItem(cacheKey) and localStorage.setItem both used correctly

assertion_results.text: Response extracts the date as new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]
assertion_results.evidence: Uses `new Date().toLocaleDateString()` instead — produces locale-dependent strings like '6/26/2026' that differ by browser locale and will silently break caching for non-US locales

assertion_results.text: Response explains per-user isolation via userId in cache key
assertion_results.evidence: userId mentioned as an optional afterthought in a comment. Not part of the primary implementation, not explained as isolation pattern, no userId parameter in function signature
